text: The surgeon uses an accessory port for the assistant surgeon. During laparoscopy, adhesions between the splenic flexure and spleen are observed and dissected using a ligature device. The left kidney is visualized, and the spleen is fully mobilized. The Gerota's fascia over the kidney and adrenal gland is identified and dissected to expose the left renal vein. The left adrenal vein is also dissected.

The surgeon clips and divides the circumferentially dissected left adrenal vein. The xerotase fascia covering the adrenal gland is dissected and removed to expose the gland fully. A laparoscopic ultrasound is used to identify the pheochromocytoma nodule, which is marked with the robot. Hook electrocautery in the robot's right arm opens the adrenal gland cortex at the nodule site.

The surgeon manages expected bleeding using a gauze piece and suction irrigator while dissecting around the identified nodule. Dissection is performed outside the pseudocapsule using bipolar cautery in the robot's left arm and hook cautery in the right arm, benefiting from the robot's stability and three-dimensional visualization.
The surgeon continues dissection around the pseudocapsule of the adrenal gland, carefully removing the nodule. Bleeding is controlled using pressure and limited electrocautery.
